title: M1 stale-tracker drain batch — 11 ISSs (1 narrow patch + 10 closure-only verifications)
target: data/wins.json (one detail_verdict_text patch on WIN-006 resolving ISS-1912) + 10 closure-only verifications for ISSs whose underlying content has been independently resolved (9 already in closed-issues.json via c8 verification or wontfix; 2 open in OI but OBE because the offending text has been removed or already corrected by prior unattributed patches).
summary_for_decider:
Stale-tracker drain batch. M1 had 27 pending tracker entries; 10 had ISSs already closed in closed-issues.json (closed 2026-05-12T09:19:48Z either by c8-verification-WIN-028 or as wontfix-duplicate). 2 more (ISS-1852, ISS-1953) are open in open-issues.json but their underlying content has already been corrected — either by prior unattributed patches (IAU URL already deep-links to IAU2006_Resol1.pdf) or by structural removal during a prior WIN-028 rewrite (v22 framing removed entirely from WIN-028 and now lives only in WIN-027 in a different argumentative role).

PATCH section — one string_replace patch:
• ISS-1912 (WIN-006.c3 H1): adds a one-phrase parenthetical to detail_verdict_text clarifying that the NOAA NGDC NP.xy file is itself a model-derived reconstruction (gufm1 pre-1900 + IGRF series for modern era) — not a raw measurement series. This pre-empts the dome-advocate question "is NP.xy raw data or a model output?" The clarification does not weaken the post-hoc argument: the NP.xy/IGRF-14 product was publicly available at the time of the dome monitor commit either way, and model-reconstructed pole-position data is exactly the kind of indexed published material the Part 3.1b trivial-transcription test is designed to catch. The patch is narrow (one parenthetical insertion, no other text affected) and the surrounding sentence already cites IGRF-14 so the additional gufm1/IGRF mention is contextually consistent.

CLOSURE-ONLY section — 11 verification closures (9 ISSs already in closed-issues, 2 OBE):
• ISS-1849 (WIN-017 rhetorical phrasing): already closed 2026-05-12 by decider as wontfix-duplicate-of-ISS-1854. The phrasing critique is preserved as ISS-1854. EXP-325 tracker entry pops as stale.
• ISS-1852 (WIN-021 IAU URL): description claims URL is https://www.iau.org/ (homepage). Spot-check of current data/wins.json:["021"].detail_evidence shows URL is in fact https://www.iau.org/static/resolutions/IAU2006_Resol1.pdf — the deep link the issue requested. Issue is OBE; the URL was already corrected by an unattributed prior patch (likely an earlier c-cycle that closed ISS-1852-equivalent under a different ID). Issue-proposal recommends close-as-OBE.
• ISS-1912 (WIN-006 NP.xy caveat): resolved by the patch above.
• ISS-1934, ISS-1935, ISS-1936, ISS-1937 (WIN-028 c6 H1-H4): all closed 2026-05-12 by c8-verification-WIN-028. The c8 patch_verification field explicitly confirms tldr_evidence rewrite, finding rewrite, steelman heading tightening, and FUSE-side operator edits (Devil's Sea anchoring, vague-reading paragraph, WMM 2025 URL) all landed. current_verdict_holds=true. EXP-338/339/340/341 tracker entries pop as stale.
• ISS-1949, ISS-1950, ISS-1951, ISS-1952 (WIN-028 c6 H1-H4 dupes): same closure event 2026-05-12 by c8-verification-WIN-028. These were apparently dupes of ISS-1934-1937 raised in a parallel re-routing pass. EXP-344/345/346/347 tracker entries pop as stale.
• ISS-1953 (WIN-028.c6 H4 v22 framing): says WIN-028 text invokes "v22 found circular disc fails on southern/cross-equator distances". Spot-check: grep "v22" data/wins.json returns 1 hit, all in WIN-027.detail_evidence, NONE in WIN-028. The v22 framing was removed from WIN-028 entirely during the c6→c7→c8 rewrite cycle. WIN-027 still cites it but in a different argumentative role (post-hoc parameter adjustment evidence — appropriate context). ISS-1953 is OBE; issue-proposal recommends close-as-OBE.

TRACKER section: 12 pending tracker entries pop with batch_owner=EXP-366: EXP-325, EXP-327, EXP-331, EXP-338, EXP-339, EXP-340, EXP-341, EXP-344, EXP-345, EXP-346, EXP-347, EXP-348.

ISSUE-PROPOSALS section: 3 written for currently-open ISSs being resolved: ISS-1852 (close-as-OBE), ISS-1912 (patch+close), ISS-1953 (close-as-OBE). No proposals for the 9 already-closed ISSs.

After this batch: tracker pending drops from 27 to 15 (12 popped, EXP-366 added as complete).

patches.old_string: field-specific published dataset (NOAA NGDC NP.xy historical pole-position records, IGRF-14)
patches.new_string: field-specific published dataset (NOAA NGDC NP.xy historical pole-position records — a model-reconstructed product blending gufm1 for the pre-1900 era and the IGRF series for the modern era, not a raw measurement file — with IGRF-14 as the current IGRF generation)
patches.rationale: Adds a one-phrase parenthetical clarifying that NP.xy is itself model-derived (gufm1 + IGRF), so a dome-advocate cannot pivot to "is NP.xy raw measurement or model output?" The clarification does not weaken the post-hoc argument because the trivial-transcription test applies to any indexed published dataset regardless of whether it is raw or reconstructed — model-reconstructed pole-position files are exactly the kind of well-indexed product the test catches. Sentence remains a single readable clause; total length added ~135 characters.
patches.verification: Pre-patch grep: target string occurs exactly once in WIN-006.detail_verdict_text (verified via node count). Post-patch verification: decider should re-grep and confirm exactly one occurrence of the new_string and zero occurrences of the old_string after applying.

closure_notes.finding: ISS-1849 was closed 2026-05-12T09:19:48.199Z by decider-2026-05-12T09-17 as wontfix-duplicate-of-ISS-1854. Tracker entry EXP-325 was not popped at closure time, leaving it stale. The duplicate closure rationale (analytical inference about "fitted backward" / "reverse-engineered" phrasing is supported by absence of any published derivation for the 20m wobble amplitude) is preserved under ISS-1854. No new analyst action required; popping EXP-325 from tracker as batch_owner=EXP-366.

ISS-1852 description claims WIN-021 IAU citation URL points to https://www.iau.org/ (homepage). Spot-check of current data/wins.json:["021"].detail_evidence shows the IAU URL is in fact https://www.iau.org/static/resolutions/IAU2006_Resol1.pdf — the IAU 2006 Resolution B1 deep link the issue requested as the preferred fix. The URL was already corrected by some prior patch cycle and the tracker entry was not popped. Recommend decider close ISS-1852 with fixed_by="OBE-already-corrected-by-prior-patch".
closure_notes.verification: grep -E "https?://[^\"\'\s>]*iau.org[^\"\'\s>]*" against WIN-021 returns exactly one URL: https://www.iau.org/static/resolutions/IAU2006_Resol1.pdf. Zero occurrences of the bare https://www.iau.org/ homepage.

WIN-028.c8.json patch_verification confirms decider commit 417a887c rewrote finding quantitative-first and tldr_evidence with kernel acknowledgement; current_verdict_holds=true. Tracker entry EXP-338 was not popped at closure time.

ISS-1935 (WIN-028 c6 INTERNAL-CONSISTENCY FRAMING MISSING) closed 2026-05-12 by c8-verification-WIN-028. The c5/c6/c7 carry-over about WIN-029 5.3% / WIN-027 R²=0.7874 / WIN-030 48.6% as dome confirmation thresholds is now explicitly handled in current WIN-028.detail_evidence with the "internal-consistency benchmark only — not as an independently valid standard" caveat. Tracker entry EXP-339 was not popped at closure time.
closure_notes.verification: closed-issues.json:ISS-1935.status="closed". WIN-028.detail_evidence contains the verbatim caveat "cite it here as an internal-consistency benchmark only — showing the dome's own framework treats single-digit residuals as confirmation — not as an independently valid standard."

closure_notes.finding: ISS-1936 (WIN-028 c6 METRIC-TYPE CONFLATION between 5.3% residual / R² / RMS reduction) closed 2026-05-12 by c8-verification-WIN-028. Current WIN-028.detail_evidence explicitly notes "these are distinct statistical measures from a residual percentage, but each reflects the dome accepting tight fits as its standard" — directly addressing the conflation concern. Tracker entry EXP-340 was not popped at closure time.
closure_notes.verification: closed-issues.json:ISS-1936.status="closed". WIN-028.detail_evidence contains the verbatim distinction "distinct statistical measures from a residual percentage, but each reflects the dome accepting tight fits as its standard."

closure_notes.finding: ISS-1937 (WIN-028 c6 v22 SELF-OWN — "v22 found circular disc fails on southern/cross-equator distances" as dome's own retrospective failure threshold could be flipped) closed 2026-05-12 by c8-verification-WIN-028. The v22 framing was removed from WIN-028 entirely during the c6→c8 rewrite — grep "v22" data/wins.json returns 1 hit, all in WIN-027.detail_evidence (different argumentative role). Tracker entry EXP-341 was not popped at closure time.
closure_notes.verification: closed-issues.json:ISS-1937.status="closed". grep v22 against WIN-028 fields returns zero hits.

closure_notes.finding: ISS-1949 (WIN-028 28/180 denominator choice unjustified — defender can ask why not 28/360 or 28/90) closed 2026-05-12 by c8-verification-WIN-028. Current WIN-028.detail_evidence now includes the explicit justification "relative to the target of 180° — the dome's claim is antipodal symmetry so the target is 180°; relative error = deviation ÷ target is the standard form — no other referent is published by the dome" — pre-empting the denominator-flip attack. Tracker entry EXP-344 was not popped at closure time. Note: this is a near-duplicate of ISS-1934.
closure_notes.verification: closed-issues.json:ISS-1949.status="closed". WIN-028.detail_evidence contains the verbatim denominator-justification clause.

closure_notes.finding: ISS-1950 (WIN-028 uses WIN-029 5.3% etc. as dome confirmation thresholds but we verdict those WINs Self-Contradicted/Misleading) closed 2026-05-12 by c8-verification-WIN-028. Same caveat as ISS-1935 applies — current text explicitly states WIN-029 is itself verdicted Self-Contradicted, and the 5.3% is cited as internal-consistency benchmark only. Near-duplicate of ISS-1935. Tracker entry EXP-345 was not popped at closure time.
closure_notes.verification: closed-issues.json:ISS-1950.status="closed". WIN-028.detail_evidence contains the parenthetical "(WIN-029 is itself verdicted Self-Contradicted in our review)".

closure_notes.finding: ISS-1951 (WIN-028 v22 evolution-log was 100% deviation; defender flips "v22 proves your standard is 100% and 15.6% passes that bar") closed 2026-05-12 by c8-verification-WIN-028. Resolved by removing v22 framing from WIN-028 entirely (same evidence as ISS-1937). Tracker entry EXP-346 was not popped at closure time.
closure_notes.verification: closed-issues.json:ISS-1951.status="closed". grep v22 against WIN-028 fields returns zero hits.

closure_notes.finding: ISS-1952 (WIN-028.c6 H3 metric-type conflation) closed 2026-05-12 by c8-verification-WIN-028. Source curmudgeon_review WIN-028.c6.json queue_id=325 H3. Same evidence as ISS-1936 (the distinct-statistical-measures caveat). Tracker entry EXP-347 was not popped at closure time.

ISS-1953 (WIN-028.c6 H4 moderate — "Text invokes v22 found circular disc fails on southern/cross-equator distances as evidence that 15.6% is near the dome's own retrospective failure threshold — but v22 was the dome's own model version, not a published tolerance") is OBE: the v22 framing was entirely removed from WIN-028 during the c6→c8 rewrite. grep "v22" data/wins.json returns 1 hit total, all in WIN-027.detail_evidence where v22 is cited in a different argumentative role (post-hoc parameter adjustment evidence — "WIN-027 (quadratic law) and WIN-030 (ellipse) directly address this" is the dome's own evolution-log admission, not a dome tolerance claim). The ISS-1953 concern does not apply to WIN-027's usage. ISS-1953 still listed as assigned-analyst in open-issues.json — recommend decider close with fixed_by="OBE-content-removed-by-c8-rewrite". Tracker entry EXP-348 pops as stale.
closure_notes.verification: grep v22 against WIN-028 fields returns zero hits. grep v22 against WIN-027.detail_evidence returns the evolution-log quote in post-hoc parameter adjustment context, not a tolerance claim.

research_notes: Drain pattern observation: the recurring c8/c-N verification-pass that closes ISSs by reference to a verified upstream fix does not currently pop the corresponding expansion-tracker entries. This is the same tracker-decoupling observed in the 2026-04-27 analyst report (tracker-staleness fallback added to Mode 2b OBE criteria). The decoupling is structural — decider closes ISSs in M1 Priority 5 / 6 (issue-disposition logic) but the tracker pop happens in M-Integrate when a new EXP is integrated. Closures that happen via c-N verification (no new EXP) leave the tracker entry orphaned.

Counter-argument considered: should we delegate the stale-tracker cleanup to a different agent (e.g., tinker) rather than have analyst pop them in M1 batches? Arguments for analyst doing it: (a) analyst has the scientific context to verify the closures were legitimate (vs. mechanically checking ISS status), (b) the M1 dispatcher fires on tracker pending count so leaving stale entries inflates the trigger metric, (c) the recent EXP-365 / ISS-1962 pattern established this as analyst work. Arguments against: (a) it dilutes the analyst Opus budget on tracker bookkeeping, (b) a mechanical popping pass would not require Opus. Compromise: do the closure verifications when they have scientific content to verify (e.g., the v22-removal check, the IAU URL deep-link check), but pure status-mirror popping (where the ISS is already in closed-issues with a wontfix that has no scientific re-check) is fair to mechanize.

Operator should consider adding a tinker action item: pipeline-level fix to have the decider's issue-closer also pop the tracker entry when it closes an ISS that has a tracker entry. This eliminates the decoupling at source. The PROP-029 safety net only catches the routing decoupling (assigned-analyst without tracker entry), not the closure decoupling (closed without tracker pop).
test_coverage: tests/run.sh — patch only modifies a prose parenthetical in WIN-006.detail_verdict_text; no numerical claims or data values changed; no test plumbing needed.
